title: Guilt Words guilt culpability remorse

pages.text:
223
Emotion Regulation Handout 6 (p. 10 of 10)
Guilt Words
guilt
culpability
remorse
apologetic
regret
sorry
Prompting Events for Feeling Guilt
•• Doing or thinking something you believe is
wrong.
•• Doing or thinking something that violates your
personal values.
•• Not doing something you said that you would
do.
•• Committing a transgression against another
person or something you value.
•• Causing harm/damage to another person or
object.
•• Causing harm/damage to yourself.
•• Being reminded of something wrong you did
in the past.
•• Other: Interpretations of Events That Prompt Feelings of Guilt
•• Thinking that your actions are to blame for
something.
•• Thinking that you behaved badly.
•• Thinking, “If only I had done something
differently... ”
•• Other:
Biological Changes and Experiences of Guilt
•• Hot, red face.
•• Jitteriness, nervousness.
•• Suffocating.
•• Other:
Expressions and Actions of Guilt
•• Trying to repair the harm, make amends for the wrongdoing, fix the damage, change the
outcome.
•• Asking for forgiveness, apologizing, confessing.
•• Giving gifts/making sacrifices to try to make up for the transgression.
•• Bowing your head; kneeling before the person.
Aftereffects of Guilt
•• Making resolutions to change.
•• Making changes in behavior.
•• Joining self-help programs.
•• Other:
Other Important Emotion Words
•• Weariness, dissatisfaction, disinclination.
•• Distress.
•• Shyness, fragility, reserve, bashfulness, coyness, reticence.
•• Cautiousness, reluctance, suspiciousness, caginess, wariness.
•• Surprise, amazement, astonishment, awe, startle, wonder.
•• Boldness, bravery, courage, determination.
•• Powerfulness, a sense of competence, capability, mastery.
•• Dubiousness, skepticism, doubtfulness.
•• Apathy, boredom, dullness, ennui, fidgetiness, impatience, indifference, listlessness.

copyright_notice: Extracted from user-provided PDF: DBT Skills Training Handouts and Worksheets, Second Edition by Marsha M. Linehan. Respect source copyright and purchaser-use limits.

ourspace_kindness_pass.title: Guilt Words guilt culpability remorse

main_doability_needs: bed-level or seated options by default
oxygen/breathing, pain, vertigo, fatigue, mobility, and recovery time respected
ADHD/autism supports: transitions, low-stimulation starts, tiny visible steps
partial completion and stopping before a crash count as success
no shame, no body blame, no pressure to push through pain or dysphoria
ADHD transition support before task demands
caregiver burnout recovery treated as care, not avoidance
protected rest, boundaries, decompression, sensory needs, and people-pleasing checks
no assumption that Jasper has unlimited caregiver capacity

household_rules: no hour block may hold more than two tasks/tools
skills may be used as one-breath or one-field versions
survival days load critical care first and skip optional worksheets
rest, boundaries, decompression, and asking for help count as care
skills are for support, not grades

universal_kindness_rules: This is a support tool, not a test.
One line, one checkbox, one breath, or one tiny step counts.
Skipping a skill because the body is unsafe, exhausted, dysregulated, or overloaded is allowed.
Do not stack this with more than one other task inside the same hour.
Survival-day version always wins over perfection.
william_accessibility_adaptations: Use bed-level, seated, voice-note, or caregiver-assisted versions when needed.
Avoid pain escalation, respiratory strain, unsafe transfers, and dysphoria-triggering language.
Break worksheets into one-field chunks; stopping before a crash is success.
jasper_accessibility_adaptations: Start with transition support before expecting focus.
Protect caregiver burnout recovery; rest and boundaries are valid completion.
Use body-doubling, timers, low-stimulation versions, and one-minute starts.
tiny_steps: Choose one sentence or tool that fits today.
Use the low-spoon version; no full worksheet is required.

routine_integration.routine_role: weekly reflection, planning, or repair tool
routine_integration.start_the_day_use: only insert on expanded days into an empty/optional block
routine_integration.recommended_block: weekly reset, conflict repair, cleaning/planning review, or emotion review
